channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Sam Altman’s thank-you to coders draws the memes
item.description: Altman expresses gratitude for people who knew how to write their code from scratch. The internet replies with salty jokes.

item.title: The FBI confirms it's buying Americans' location data
item.description: During a Senate hearing, FBI Director Kash Patel confirmed that his agency has bought information that could be used to track individuals' movement and location. "We do purchase commercially available information that’s consistent with the Constitution and the laws under the Electronic Communications Privacy Act, and it has led to some valuable intelligence for us," he said. Law enforcement is required to obtain a warrant in order to get location data from cell service providers following the Carpenter v United States ruling from 2018. But why bother with all that hassle when they can just buy...

item.title: Rabbit threatens to revive the legendary Sony Vaio P netbook as a $500 dedicated Linux "vibe coding machine" to take on Apple's uber popular MacBook Neo — but it won't be fast
item.description: Rabbit’s Cyberdeck revives the Vaio P concept, offering a portable, Linux-based vibe coding device with affordable pricing and customizable hardware.

item.title: 'You pick something heavy up and you walk with it': A celebrity PT recommends this back-to-basics move as the best strength exercise for over 50s
item.description: How a single gym exercise — the farmer's carry — can prepare your body for life after 50.

item.title: These small and stylish wireless stereo speakers pack in Hi-Res connectivity, turntable phono support, HDMI, aptX and more — and all at a seriously tempting price
item.description: Elipson's new active speakers have extensive wired and wireless connectivity including an MM phono stage and aptX HD

item.title: A Meta agentic AI sparked a security incident by acting without permission
item.description: The Information reported that an AI agent within Meta took unauthorized action that led to an employee creating a security breach at the social company last week. According to the publication, an employee used an in-house agentic AI to analyze a query from a second employee on an internal forum. The AI agent posted a response to the second employee with advice even though the first person did not direct it to do so. The second employee took the agent's recommended action, sparking a domino effect that led to some engineers having access to Meta systems that they shouldn't have permission to se...

item.title: Government Registers Aliens.Gov Domain as Next Distraction From Everything Else
item.description: The truth is out there (on the internet).

item.title: Nothing Headphone A vs. Headphone 1: Which Pair of ANC Wireless Headphones Wins?
item.description: Nothing versus Nothing is really something.

item.title: ‘Witch Hat Atelier’ Is Simply Magical
item.description: Bug Films' anime take on Kamome Shirahama's beloved fantasy series, due on Crunchyroll this April, was well worth the wait.

item.title: After 80 hours of Crimson Desert I'm thirsty for more of its breathtaking open-world and enthralling set pieces — it just needs a few tweaks in some areas to become a masterpiece
item.description: Crimson Desert provides a fantastic open-world experience, which thrives in puzzle-solving, finding surprises, and combat that rivals the best out there.

item.title: The FBI is buying Americans’ location data
item.description: FBI director Kash Patel admitted that the agency is buying location data that can be used to track people's movements. Unlike information obtained from cell phone providers, this data can be accessed without a warrant - and used to track anyone. "We do purchase commercially available information that's consistent with the Constitution and the laws […]...

item.title: Kash Patel Admits the FBI is Buying Private Data on Americans
item.description: Sen. Ron Wyden called it an "outrageous end run around the Fourth Amendment."
